proposed_title: Citation-compliance audit: bring pre-2026-04-18 citations into 'as of [date]' compliance
description: Curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 hole #3 spot-checks (Section 4.1 EXP-182 update; Section 2b.5 apr18 update) found that several citations to dome content in our review do NOT carry explicit 'as of [date]' tags — they cite by URL alone. EXP-398 softens the part8b self-claim from 'already in force' to 'going forward' and points to this issue. The audit pass should: (1) enumerate all dome-source citations in data/sections.json that lack an 'as of [date]' label; (2) for each, determine the citation date from monitor/changes/ if not obvious; (3) patch the citation in-place to add the label; (4) report a compliance summary (N citations checked, M patched, K already-compliant) for the public record. This is mechanical, high-volume work better suited to a dedicated EXP cluster or a structured curmudgeon pass; the prose change in EXP-398 should not block on it.
proposed_assignee: decider routes to analyst-baby for citation enumeration (verification-class) and to a follow-up EXP for the bulk patch; consider splitting across multiple EXPs if the citation set is large.
why_filing_as_separate_issue: Scope and isolation. The audit touches potentially hundreds of citations across data/sections.json; running it inline with the EXP-398 prose-softening would produce a brittle multi-hundred-line diff. The clean sequence is (1) soften the false-positive claim now [EXP-398], (2) file the audit as a tracked issue [this proposal], (3) execute the audit in a follow-up EXP cluster.
